Test Scenario: Open the Delete account screen from Settings
Test Steps:
1. Go to Settings
2. Tap the "Delete account" link at the bottom of the screen
Expected Result: 1–2. The "Are you sure you want to delete your account?" screen is shown with the reason list and the line "Select all that apply:"

Test Scenario: All 8 reasons are displayed with the correct text
Test Steps: 1. Observe the reason list
Expected Result: 1. Exactly 8 checkboxes shown: trouble creating submissions / approval too long / no longer work on site / created by mistake / have another account / technical issues / privacy concern / Other

Test Scenario: Select a single reason
Test Steps: 1. Tick the "I no longer work on the site" checkbox
Expected Result: 1. The checkbox is ticked; the "Delete account" button is enabled

Test Scenario: Select multiple reasons at once (multi-select)
Test Steps:
1. Tick "Submission approval takes too long"
2. Tick "I am experiencing technical issues with the app"
Expected Result: 1–2. Both checkboxes are ticked at the same time (multi-select works — "Select all that apply")

Test Scenario: Select all 8 reasons
Test Steps: 1. Tick all 8 checkboxes one by one
Expected Result: 1. All 8 checkboxes are ticked, no error

Test Scenario: Untick a reason
Test Steps:
1. Tick "I created this account by mistake"
2. Tap the same checkbox again to untick it
Expected Result:
1. The checkbox is ticked
2. The checkbox is correctly unticked

Test Scenario: No reason selected → cannot submit (Negative)
Test Steps:
1. Do not tick any reason
2. Tap "Delete account"
Expected Result: 1–2. The "Delete account" button is disabled OR an error requiring at least one reason is shown; the confirmation modal does not open

Test Scenario: Tick "Other" → the details field appears
Test Steps: 1. Tick the "Other" checkbox
Expected Result: 1. A "Please enter details" text field (placeholder "Please enter here") appears right below "Other"

Test Scenario: Tick "Other" + enter details text
Test Steps:
1. Tick "Other"
2. Enter text into the details field
Expected Result: 1–2. The entered text is shown in the details field

Test Scenario: Tick "Other" with an empty details field → error (per UI)
Test Steps:
1. Tick "Other"
2. Leave the details field empty
3. Tap "Delete account"
Expected Result: 3. Error "Please enter the details here" is shown, the modal does not open. ⚠️ CONFLICT: the requirement says "optional text input" but the UI enforces it as required — needs BA confirmation

Test Scenario: Untick "Other" → the details field is hidden
Test Steps:
1. Tick "Other", enter text
2. Untick "Other"
Expected Result: 2. The details field is hidden; the previously entered content is handled consistently (cleared/kept — needs BA confirmation)

Test Scenario: "Other details" field — maximum length (BVA)
Test Steps:
1. Enter 500 characters
2. Enter up to the 501st character
Expected Result:
1. Accepts 500 characters
2. Blocks the 501st character. *Requirement does not define the max — assumed 500*

Test Scenario: Tap "Delete account" → the confirmation modal opens
Test Steps: 1. Tap the "Delete account" button
Test Data: Reason: "I no longer work on the site"
Expected Result: 1. The "Permanently delete your account?" modal is shown with a warning + "Delete account" and "Cancel" buttons

Test Scenario: The modal shows a clear warning message
Test Steps: 1. Read the modal content
Expected Result: 1. The modal clearly states: the account will be permanently deleted within 24h and can be cancelled/recovered during that time

Test Scenario: Tap "Cancel" on the modal → no submission
Test Steps: 1. Tap the "Cancel" button on the modal
Expected Result: 1. The modal closes, returns to the reason selection screen; account stays Active; NO deletion request is created

Test Scenario: Tap "Delete account" on the modal → submit succeeds (Happy Path)
Test Steps: 1. Tap the "Delete account" button on the modal
Expected Result: 1. The request is submitted; navigates to the "Deletion Scheduled" screen; account status → "Pending Deletion"

Test Scenario: The request is added to the Message Queue (24h delay)
Test Steps:
1. Submit the request
2. Check the Message Queue via a backend tool
Expected Result: 2. The queue has 1 deletion job, delivery delay = 24h, payload contains worker_id + reason + timestamp

Test Scenario: Account status changes to "Pending Deletion"
Test Steps:
1. Submit the request
2. Check the status in the DB
Expected Result: 2. status = "Pending Deletion"

Test Scenario: Submit while the network is disconnected (Edge)
Test Steps:
1. Enable Airplane Mode
2. Tap "Delete account" on the modal
Expected Result: 2. A network error is shown; the request is NOT submitted; status stays Active; a Retry option is available

Test Scenario: The "Deletion Scheduled" screen is shown after submitting
Test Steps: 1. Observe the screen after tapping "Delete account" on the modal
Expected Result: 1. The "Deletion Scheduled" screen is shown with a countdown timer and a "Cancel Account Deletion" button

Test Scenario: The countdown timer initializes correctly to ~24h
Test Steps: 1. Observe the countdown timer right after submitting
Expected Result: 1. The timer shows approximately "23h 59m" and starts counting down to 0

Test Scenario: The countdown timer counts down in real time
Test Steps:
1. Note the timer value
2. Wait 5 minutes, then observe again
Expected Result: 2. The timer has decreased by ~5 minutes (e.g. from "23h 59m" → "23h 54m")

Test Scenario: The 24h delay is calculated accurately
Test Steps:
1. Submit the request
2. Check the scheduled_deletion_at field
Expected Result: 2. scheduled_deletion_at = 2026-05-22 10:00:00 (exactly +24h)

Test Scenario: Close the app and reopen during the Pending Deletion period
Test Steps:
1. Submit deletion
2. Fully close the app
3. Reopen the app and log in
Expected Result: 3. The app shows the "Deletion Scheduled" screen again, with the timer continuing correctly + the "Cancel Account Deletion" button

Test Scenario: Tap "Cancel Account Deletion" → the "Keep your account?" dialog opens
Test Steps: 1. Tap the "Cancel Account Deletion" button
Expected Result: 1. The "Keep your account?" dialog is shown with "Keep My Account" and "Continue Deletion" buttons

Test Scenario: Tap "Keep My Account" → cancellation succeeds (Happy Path)
Pre-Condition: The "Keep your account?" dialog is open
Test Steps: 1. Tap the "Keep My Account" button
Expected Result: 1. The deletion is cancelled; navigates to the "My Wallet" screen with the "Deletion request cancelled" banner; account Active

Test Scenario: Tap "Continue Deletion" → keep the deletion scheduled
Pre-Condition: The "Keep your account?" dialog is open
Test Steps: 1. Tap the "Continue Deletion" button
Expected Result: 1. The dialog closes, stays on the "Deletion Scheduled" screen, the timer keeps running, the deletion is still scheduled

Test Scenario: The request is removed from the Message Queue when cancelled
Pre-Condition: account Pending Deletion, a job exists in the queue
Test Steps:
1. Cancel Account Deletion → Keep My Account
2. Check the Message Queue
Expected Result: 2. The corresponding deletion job is removed / the cancel event is processed; the queue no longer holds a job to delete this account

Test Scenario: Account status returns to Active after cancellation
Test Steps:
1. Cancel Account Deletion → Keep My Account
2. Check the status in the DB and UI
Expected Result: 2. status = "Active" in both DB and UI

Test Scenario: The "Deletion request cancelled" banner is shown on My Wallet
Test Steps: 1. Observe the "My Wallet" screen after cancelling
Expected Result: 1. A confirmation banner "Deletion request cancelled / account active again" is shown at the top of the screen

Test Scenario: Cancel close to the 24h mark — inner boundary (BVA: timer ~00h01m)
Test Steps: 1. Tap "Cancel Account Deletion" → "Keep My Account"
Test Data: Cancel when ~1 minute is left on the timer
Expected Result: 1. Cancellation still succeeds, the account returns to Active

Test Scenario: Cannot cancel after 24h — outer boundary (BVA)
Test Steps: 1. Try to open the app / log in with the deleted account
Expected Result: 1. No "Deletion Scheduled" screen / Cancel button; the account is permanently deleted, login fails

Test Scenario: Cancel while the network is disconnected (Edge)
Pre-Condition: The "Keep your account?" dialog is open
Test Steps:
1. Enable Airplane Mode
2. Tap "Keep My Account"
Expected Result: 2. A network error is shown; cancellation is NOT performed; account stays Pending Deletion; Retry available

Test Scenario: Submit deletion again after cancelling → a new 24h window
Test Steps:
1. Go to Settings → "Delete account"
2. Select a reason → modal → "Delete account"
3. Check the timer + scheduled_deletion_at
Expected Result: 3. A new request is created; a fresh "23h 59m" timer; scheduled_deletion_at = 2026-05-22 15:00:00

Test Scenario: The account is automatically deleted after 24h if not cancelled (Happy Path)
Pre-Condition: submitted a full 24h ago, not cancelled, timer at 0
Test Steps:
1. Wait until the 24h mark
2. Check the account in the system
Expected Result: 2. The backend processes the queue event; the account is permanently deleted from the system

Test Scenario: Authentication access is revoked after deletion
Test Steps:
1. Call an API with the old access token/session
2. Try to log in again
Expected Result:
1. The API returns 401 — the token is revoked
2. Login fails

Test Scenario: The account is NOT deleted before the 24h mark (BVA: inner boundary)
Test Steps: 1. At the 23h mark, check the account
Expected Result: 1. The account still exists, status = "Pending Deletion", not yet deleted

Test Scenario: Log in with an already-deleted account
Test Steps:
1. Enter the email + password of the deleted account
2. Tap "Log in"
Expected Result: 2. Login fails, "Account not found" / an equivalent message is shown

Test Scenario: The backend handles a queue event error → retry / dead-letter
Pre-Condition: Simulate a service error while processing the deletion job
Test Steps:
1. Push the job to processing time, simulate a service error
2. Observe the queue behaviour
Expected Result: 2. The job is retried per policy or moved to the dead-letter queue; the job is NOT lost; the account is not stuck in a hanging state

Test Scenario: The reasons (multi-select) are stored for analytics
Pre-Condition: Worker submits deletion with multiple reasons
Test Steps:
1. Tick 2 reasons
2. Submit deletion
3. Check the analytics/deletion_reasons table
Test Data: "Submission approval takes too long" + "I am concerned about privacy or data usage"
Expected Result: 3. The record stores all selected reasons + worker_id + timestamp

Test Scenario: The "Other" reason + text content is stored
Pre-Condition: Worker submits with the "Other" reason and text entered
Test Steps:
1. Tick "Other", enter text
2. Submit deletion
3. Check the data store
Expected Result: 3. The record stores reason = "Other" and reason_text = the entered content

Test Scenario: The cancellation event is tracked for analytics
Test Steps:
1. Cancel Account Deletion → Keep My Account
2. Check the analytics/cancellation_events table
Expected Result: 2. A cancellation event record exists: worker_id, timestamp, the request's original reason

Test Scenario: The worker receives a notification when submitting a deletion request
Test Steps:
1. Submit the request
2. Check the push notification + email
Expected Result: 2. The worker receives a confirmation: the deletion request is scheduled and can be cancelled within 24h

Test Scenario: The worker receives a notification when cancellation succeeds
Test Steps:
1. Cancel Account Deletion → Keep My Account
2. Check the notification + email
Expected Result: 2. The worker receives a "Your account deletion request has been cancelled, your account remains active" notification

Test Scenario: Notification/email when the account is permanently deleted
Test Steps: 1. Check the email sent to the registered address
Expected Result: 1. A "Your account has been permanently deleted" confirmation email is sent

Test Scenario: Valid state transition: Active → Pending Deletion → Active (cancel flow)
Test Steps:
1. Submit deletion (select reason → modal → Delete account)
2. Cancel Account Deletion → Keep My Account
Expected Result:
1. status = Pending Deletion
2. status = Active; the account works normally

Test Scenario: Valid state transition: Active → Pending Deletion → Deleted (automatic flow)
Test Steps:
1. Submit deletion
2. Do not cancel, wait past 24h
Expected Result:
1. status = Pending Deletion
2. status = Deleted, the account is permanently deleted

Test Scenario: Cannot create a second deletion request while Pending Deletion
Test Steps: 1. Go to Settings
Expected Result: 1. The "Delete account" link is unavailable or leads straight to the "Deletion Scheduled" screen; only 1 deletion job exists in the queue

Test Scenario: Log in again while Pending Deletion
Test Steps: 1. Log in again with the account currently in Pending Deletion
Expected Result: 1. Login succeeds, the app navigates to the "Deletion Scheduled" screen (timer + "Cancel Account Deletion")
